title: [lichen_util.h:54] secure_zero returns without zeroing if ptr is NULL
description:
Contract: secure_zero should securely wipe memory.
Violation: Lines 58-62 check for NULL, print warning, and return early. The warning goes to printk but there is no error return value (void function). Caller cannot know the wipe failed.
Impact: If called with NULL, sensitive data is not wiped. The ASSERT and printk provide some visibility but are not programmatic. However, the __ASSERT macro may halt in debug builds.
Mitigation: Return type is void, so cannot return error. The printk warning is best-effort. Callers should never pass NULL - this is defensive code for bug detection.
close_reason: Correct defensive behavior: secure_zero cannot zero NULL. The __ASSERT catches caller bugs in debug builds, printk warning catches them in release builds, and early return prevents UB (NULL dereference). No code change needed.
